text:
I ordered some nitro-coated seeds from another company and also these from Eretz to try out.
I coated my yard with a sprinkling of peat moss across the yard where there are more noticeable bald spots (i was afraid i sprinkled too much of a layer of peat moss on top of the seeds in some spots so I also sprinkled some seeds on top of the peat moss afterwards and lightly got them into the soil by spraying into the peat moss on a 'shower' setting from my hose).
I got really good germination rates from these seeds and covered alot of area with the 5lbs I bought.
I like how you can just throw onto the soil and they basically self-spread without need for a seed-spreader since the seeds are very round n' bouncy.
I can't really compare to the coated nitro ones yet as I just tried those out on another part of the yard and they haven't come up yet. But doesn't matter, both probably give really good germination rates.
This bag has a ziplock feature which the other bag didnt so I'll rate this one higher.
The other brand almost had some spillage accidents since there is not ziplock at the top of the bag.
These seeds are very yellow colored so its easier to see where they have spread out (the other nitro-coated brand has gray-colored seeds so its a bit harder to see where you spread them).

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with the product's germination rates, ease of use (self-spread without seed-spreader), and overall performance. The mention of a higher rating due to the ziplock feature also indicates positive experience.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la buena germinación de las semillas, la facilidad de uso y la calidad del producto. Se aprecia una comparación favorable con otro producto.

phi4-mini.justificacion: Obtuvieron buenos resultados de germinación, cobertura amplia con los 5lbs y fácil dispersión sin necesidad de un sembrador.

If you are looking for grass seed make sure to look through the options that are available and pick the one that best suits the area of the country that you live in to provide optimal results.

text:
This batch of seeds is not sprouting quite as well as other brands I've used, but considering how much seed you get for the price, it is fine. It seems like it only sprouts about half the seeds.

I use about a tablespoon of seeds soaked overnight in a little indoor pot for my cats to nom on at will. It takes about a week for the grass to grow to the point where the cats won't just tear it right out and then, watered two or three times a week, lasts for three weeks or so before being either dried out or chewed down.

Overall, this was an acceptable purchase, for the price.

I first soaked a cup of them overnight and then angled them upside down in a mason jar with a sprout lid while rinsing them every morning and night. I did this for about two days or until little white shoots appeared.

text:
Firstly, yes - we love venison, and would love to have some of our local Bambi friends for dinner, if it weren't for the small matter of their residing in a nature conservancy. That said, even though we would not benefit in any direct way from the nutrition provided to our local deer by this mix, we decided to give them an alternative to our yew trees for their Thanksgiving meal.

My husband diligently sewed the seed mix, by hand, in every open track and clearing, trusting to the late August rains to get it started. And then we had a mini drought. No rain for weeks. That was a waste of time, we thought. But yesterday we took a long walk to sew some braccia seeds we had on hand, and what a pleasant surprise! Sprouts covering every one of our selected areas with a pleasing fuzz of bright green! This stuff is amazing. I would reccommend it to any one, whatever their prospects of reward or motivation.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña describe una experiencia satisfactoria y positiva con el producto venison mix. La persona menciona la belleza de los brotes que emergieron después del sembrado, lo cual sugiere un buen resultado y una satisfacción general.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña expresa satisfacción con el producto y su rendimiento, destacando la calidad de las semillas y la facilidad de siembra.

phi4-mini.justificacion: My husband diligently sewed the seed mix by hand in every open track and clearing; no rain for weeks was a waste. But yesterday's sprouts covering selected areas with bright green fuzz were amazing, recommending it to anyone.

text:
All of these negative ratings can't be wrong. This stuff is a joke. Bought 2 and both of the sprayers leaked. Got soaked applying it and when the instructions say the green die "might" get in your skin, "might" is a drastic understatement. I'd pretty much guarantee it. And it's not a simple wash your hands with dish soap solution either.
After all of that, following the instructions 100%, I have yet to see a single grain of grass. It's been 30 days. And the customer service number listed is always busy or I'm on hold for, on the last call, 37 minutes before I gave up. Total scam!

text: Turf type tall fescue (TTTF)is highly recommended by most state AG extension depts for lawns in the so-called transition zone, due to its drought tolerance and quick establishment. However, be aware that it does have some limitations.  The drought tolerance, which comes from its ability to root deeply, can be poor in clay soil, which is very common  all over the country, but particularly in the Mid-Atlantic region, much of which is in the transition zone. The color is a good dark green, but the blades are not as fine as Kentucky bluegrass, and they tend to get even wider as the grass ages. You also need to overseed bare spots constantly, because it is a bunch type grass that does not spread by rhizomes the way KBG does. It is also a little more prone to fungus infections than KBG, a consideration in the humid parts of the country. Finally, it does not really go dormant in high heat/drought the way KBG does -- it survives because it finds water in the deep roots. If the roots are not deep, like in clay soil, it will be toast above about 90F. So I would say this -- if you do not have clay soil, this is going to be a great choice. But if your soil usually feels brick hard in summer, meaning it has a lot of clay, I'd try either KBG or perennial ryegrass, which do much better in compacted soils.

text:
I've been reading reviews on all the Brands of Grass Seed and even though I read a lot of bad reviews for ALL the Brands, I did read some good reviews too.

With that said, I decided to go for this product. My back yard is full of dog urine burns, due to my Lil Chihuahua, and some bare spots as well. I thought I'd do a test and just sprinkle the seeds on top of the dirt and grass, instead of burying the seeds with dirt. I watered twice a day, once in the morning and once at night. 5 days later, I had baby grass seedlings growing. I finally have a green backyard, without bare spots. Also, every time my doggy goes pee, I sprinkle seeds over the spot where he went pee and that makes the grass keep growing and staying green. I am no longer skeptical about this Brand and product. I'm buying another bag right now and will only be using Scott's products.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with the product, mentioning that it successfully grew grass in their backyard despite previous issues caused by dog urine. The reviewer also states they are now a fan of this brand and will only use Scott's products moving forward.

text: This seed sprouted super quickly and grew in totally by 3 weeks.  I grew it along side Scotts EZ Seed Fescue and this has out performed EZ seed in every area so far. I seeded a totally bare, tilled dirt lawn in May, San Diego ( mostly overcast weather too).  This seed was fully germinated on day 5 and by day 10 it fully covered the soil.  The EZ seed still has not fully germinated by day 21, seemed to germinate at different rates.  I am cautious about the lasting power of the Jonathan Green Seeds since it is primarily an annual grass (59% annual)  But for a spring summer lawn, the jonathan green has a much greater germ rate, speed and coverage than the EZ seed.  Was able to mow it at 21 days at a high setting.  Folks who spent a ton on sod are in awe of this seed on my lawn.  After years of using major brands with spotty results, this product swoops in with a great price and effectiveness.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The review is overwhelmingly positive, highlighting the rapid germination and coverage of Jonathan Green Seeds compared to Scotts EZ Seed Fescue. The reviewer mentions that this seed performed exceptionally well in terms of speed, coverage, and overall effectiveness for a spring summer lawn. Additionally, it was successfully mowed at high settings after 21 days, which is noted as a significant achievement given the brand's reputation for inconsistent results.

text: I had looked into this when my indoor house cat was having some digestive issues. The photos I took were of the first order. The grass was easy to grow, very vibrant, and my cat was immediately interested. I do believe it helped calm his digestive tract, along with a probiotic. However, I noticed the grass wilts very quickly, and then my cat would not eat it. I ordered it a second time because I felt it was worth the cost. The second order (which I didn't take photos of) was not as green or healthy looking as my first order, and my cat was not as interested. That was the reason for the deduction of a star. Overall, I would recommend this product as I believe it helped my cat. Due to its shelf life, it would not be a regular purchase, but justifies buying since it is not too expensive. I would recommend to any owners where their cat may be having a rough patch after antibiotics (as in the situation of my cat), excessive hairballs, or thrown up a bit, that this can be a great product for their cat's health, especially if they are an indoor cat.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer mentions that the grass helped calm their cat's digestive issues and improved its overall health. They also state that it is worth the cost due to its shelf life, which justifies a recommendation for owners facing similar issues.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la efectividad del producto para aliviar los problemas digestivos de los gatos y su valor en situaciones específicas. A pesar de algunos inconvenientes, se recomienda por su utilidad.

text: I have had Zoysia in my back yard for years and have been impressed with it's draught tolerance and thick green carpet.  This year I made the decision to change the front yard over to Zoysia.  There is a lot of information both on this web-site & the internet in general on recommended prep prior to seeding. Highly recommend you do a search and use the information available prior to planting.  For my part I used Roundup to kill the existing lawn and weeds - waited 6 weeks [as recommended] then spread a mixture of compost & top soil over my yard. Once I leveled this off I planted the seed using a Scotts spreader. Unlike others on this web site, I found the coverage worked pretty close to package specifications. I had 8,000 Square feet to plant and used 4 bags. I live in Maryland, outside Annapolis.  Recommendations were to plant when the temperature got warm [unlike other seed, where you plan in early Spring - Zoysia likes it hot]. I planted on June 4th, after 10 days I began seeing some germination. Package says 17-21 days for germination, I experienced 10- 17 days. I should note that I watered twice a day - a good soaking; I made sure the soil never dried out. I'm coming up on three weeks since I planted; research suggest that at 8 weeks you should have a pretty good stand of grass, 12 weeks the grass will start forming runners, lawn should be fully developed in 5 months. Another reviewer writes that Zoysia takes patience - I agree. The upside is: If you generally do not water your lawn in the summer and find that by July / August it is dead or dying - Zoysia may be your [...]

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with their experience, mentioning positive aspects such as the product's performance in both back and front yards, thorough preparation before planting, good coverage of seed, and overall improvement in appearance. The reviewer also notes that they agree with other reviewers who suggest patience for growth.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña describe una experiencia positiva con la siembra de Zoysia, destacando su tolerancia a sequía y la cobertura efectiva del producto. El autor destaca la importancia de la preparación previa al plantado y la paciencia necesaria para lograr un buen resultado.

phi4-mini.justificacion: La reseña destaca la satisfacción con el rendimiento del Zoysia, su tolerancia al calor y las recomendaciones de preparación. La experiencia personal refleja una buena germinación temprana y desarrollo prometedor en los primeros meses.

text: I purchased two different brands just to see which brand would win. Pennington not only wins, it grew with nothing but rain. The new grass seed sprouted within days, and has already filled in a few thin spots. It also helped that I aerated the entire lawn before planting the seed. It rained for a few days, so mother nature helped. Since it was already starting to grow, I ignored it, making this seasons reseeding effortless. This is the easiest seed I've ever used. The other brand "S" needed a LOT more attention, and pampering to get going, and isn't as rich in color, nor did it take off the way this one did. I'll buy Pennington grass seed again in the Spring, and next Fall, since I overseed biannually..

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The review is overwhelmingly positive, highlighting successful growth of the grass seed within days after planting. The reviewer mentions that Pennington's product performed exceptionally well compared to another brand, which needed more attention and pampering. The ease of use and overall satisfaction with the product are evident in this detailed account.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la facilidad de uso y el buen resultado del producto, comparándolo favorablemente con otro.

Brother in law thought we had bad seed as he didn't see the growth when he though it should be, then he read the packaging. We are re-planting 5 acres of pasture. The horses will be happy to have good grass again.
I can only give 2 starsUpdate 5/27/15 Pretty disappointed at this point much of the seed is still just laying on the ground. What a bummer all this work, time & water spent on this only to have to now either replant, or apply a good weed and feed to hopefully will be able to pill ahead of the weeds. I have to go in and spot spray specifically on some weeds like Thistle, Nettles and to think I spent over 200.00, I guess I will begin to search out new seed ( pacific northwest ) friendly!

text:
Small bag of seeds, but packed tightly. Note that these took a full month to arrive!
To germinate, I layered some wet newspaper with the seeds in between on a cookie sheet. I sprayed the paper twice a day to keep it damp and stored it in a dark place in the house. After just two days they started sprouting, and I planted them after 5 days. Almost every single seed germinated! My kitty loves the taste and fights to get at the pot, so you may want to keep it in a closed room away from your kitty or else it won't last long!

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña indica satisfacción general con el producto. La germinación exitosa de todos los semillas, la rápida aparición de las plantas y la aceptación del animal de compañía son señales claras de una experiencia positiva.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la alta tasa de germinación y la facilidad de uso, con un enfoque positivo en la experiencia personal.

phi4-mini.justificacion: Satisfacción general con el producto; casi todos los semillas germinaron y se plantaron después de solo cinco días.

text:
This is amazing. We planted late Thursday night (put 2 of the 3 kits in a 6" ceramic pot). Left photo it's barely starting to sprout on Saturday afternoon. Middle photo is already a couple inches just one day later on Sunday night! And the right photo is Tuesday night. So big after just 5 days. We put it by the cats food (less light) on Monday and it's still growing like crazy. I would have loved to see a time lapse.

The cats aren't super interested in it but I don't care. We're planting a big bag of similar seeds in a larger pot and I hope they grow as well.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña describe un producto que ha crecido de manera impresionante en tan solo 5 días, con una evolución visible desde la noche del jueves hasta el lunes. La satisfacción general es clara y positiva, destacando tanto la rápida expansión como la persistencia del crecimiento después de la introducción en un lugar menos iluminado para los gatos.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la rápida germinación y crecimiento del producto, con resultados sorprendentes en solo 5 días. La satisfacción del usuario es evidente.

phi4-mini.justificacion: La reseña expresa una experiencia positiva con el crecimiento rápido del producto, incluso en condiciones menos ideales.

text:
These packets to patch bare spots work okay. I was extra lazy and just waited for a week when we had a lot of days of rain and tossed these on some of my bare spots. I did go out and spread the seed/soil/conditioner around a bit. The soil has stayed moist for 10 days to two weeks and now I see some fine grass sprouting. So these are okay; they just don’t wow me any more than other patch methods I have used that are less expensive.

I will say that there is enough conditioner and soil that they don’t wash away in steady rain. But all in all very similar to other patching mixes. If I want to do more patching, I’ll probably compare prices and buy the most economical one.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña indica una opinión mixta y no muestra ninguna evidencia de insatisfacción ni satisfacción general. text: This grass seed was the best out of 4 versions used on lawn in Maine.  Other versions had too much filler (well beyond usual 50%) and weed seeds.  Other reviewers mention 3 week’s for green and pretty much that was the case here if not sooner. Used in both sunny and shade locations with no problem.  Did cover seed with good layer of hay and watered to keep areas damp.  This was dryest summer in a long time.  We’re having a very cold winter so there is the hope that the new grass appears healthy in the spring.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña indica que el producto fue el mejor de las cuatro versiones probadas en Maine, con una cobertura óptima y un crecimiento rápido. Los otros productos mencionados tenían demasiado polvo (más del 50%) y semillas de malezas. La experiencia general es satisfactoria, especialmente en condiciones de sol y sombra, demostrando buen funcionamiento incluso en la temporada más seca de los últimos años.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la calidad del producto, con una buena tasa de germinación y un rendimiento satisfactorio en condiciones adversas. El usuario expresa satisfacción con el resultado final.

phi4-mini.justificacion: La semilla de césped fue la mejor entre las cuatro versiones usadas en el césped del Maine. Otras versiones tenían demasiado relleno (más allá del usualmente 50%) y semillas de maleza. Sin embargo, esta versión se desarrolló a tiempo como mencionan otros revisores. Se usó tanto en lugares soleados como sombreados sin problemas. Cubrió la semilla con una buena capa de heno y mantuvo las áreas húmedas al regarlas. Fue el verano más seco del que recordamos.

Oh it comes up and then it dies right off. Works fine only really locations that aren't affected by the dog pee. The old style I never had a problem reseeding these area.

text:
Here are 2 before and after pics. This seed germinated nicely and quickly. I am in Northern Virginia, and let the summer destroy my lawn as I did not follow a good watering routine. I dethatched, aerated and over-seeded on September 3rd 2019, The pics shows the new grass after the first mow on Sept 24th 2019, in 21 days my grass was thick and healthy. I highly recommend this grass seed.

**make sure you do the prep work before seeding, also you must water the seed/lawn at least twice a day for 15-30 minutes to keep the ground moist to get the best results..

text: I live in zone 7 and have been looking for a lawn substitute. Last year I experimented with white clover and though I like the clover, it does get tall enough so that it needs to be mowed. It's low to the ground, very pretty, and so far no white flowers. I plan to use it in both the front and back yard. I'm glad I tried it.

text:
I'm a novice at growing grass. My grass was dug up due to construction work to add some pipes to the main sewage line. The gardener was charging 300 to install sod, but I didnt want to spend the money. This can only cost 10 dollars so I thought I'd give it a try first.

I didn't quite follow the directions on the can. I watered the soil, sprinkled the seeds liberally, and stepped on the seeds to embed them into the soil. The next day, I added organic fertilizer granules on top. The local store didn't have organic dirt fertilizer in a small bag so I had to buy a small bag of the granules, the kind used for tomatoes and herbs. After 2 weeks, the grass seems to be growing quite well.

I also used it on another section that was dug up and it is not growing quite as well, but I do see growth. In this particular area, I didn't step on the seeds so they layed on top of the soil. I think in order for the seeds to germinate, they have to be in contact with a source that will stay wet, otherwise the sun will dry them out.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña muestra una mezcla de opiniones positivas y negativas. La persona menciona que el producto parece estar creciendo bien después de dos semanas, pero también señala un problema en la parte del jardín donde no se estaban colocando los semillas correctamente. Sin embargo, la reseña generalmente es constructiva y muestra una experiencia mixta con algunas áreas mejoradas.

text:
After laying the grass seed, only a week later you can see some subtle changes within the seeds and notice some growth. In another couple weeks, I will come back and update with photos to show the actual growth. We planted in an area that was roots and weeds, so it will be interesting to see how much grass grows and how long it stays. The seed bag is very easy to open and not overly heavy. I did not use a spreader as it was a small area. I was able to use a measuring cup and spread it out where needed. It definitely was over seeded in areas, but since this is a section that has never grown grass, it can't hurt to start. Also, for $29 at the time of review, it is a solid price.

I will update again in a couple weeks with progress photos!

text: I have pretty much a full yard of clay soil and this product germinated well. I only roughed up the first 2 inches of soil, with a pull behind leveler attached to my ATV, and then just spread the seed with a hand spreader in a longitudinal and horizontal pattern. I did not cover seed with any hay, straw or the like and it grew awesome. I just raked the seed into the soil with a landscaping rake. (I highly recommend tilling the ground to a depth of at least 4 inches to help with the germination of strong roots).It has been three months and the snow has fallen. Up to the point of snow fall I would say 90 percent of my yard has grass growth. It still has retained its color long after my neighbors blue grass has browned due to extremely cold temperatures. I have a third of an acre and live 30 miles north of SLC, Utah. Utah is the second driest state in the country so I wanted to have a ground cover that wouldn't be to much of a drain on our water resources. I think I have found my water conservation goals with this product. It does have a nice dark green color, but is a few shades lighter in color than the rest of the blue grass lawns in the neighborhood.  I know that it will be more drought resistant so my lawn will be green during the summer whereas my neighbors will be wilting in the desert sun and heat. I used two 50 pound bags for my 1/3 Acre plot, and then bought a 20 lb bag after germination for spot seeding.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with the product's performance in various aspects. The soil was not covered with hay or straw, and it grew well without additional care. The grass has retained its color even after harsh winter conditions typical of Utah, which is a significant achievement for a ground cover that requires minimal maintenance. Additionally, the reviewer mentions finding this product suitable for their water conservation goals due to its drought resistance.

Además, destaca los beneficios para la conservación del agua en condiciones secas.

text: I've struggled with having a nice lawn all season long  for years.  Living in the northeast, it can be a challenge in the dog days of summer because a lot of my lawn would go dormant.  My landscaper told me about Jonathan Green Ultra and I saw what it did for my lawn.  The trick is, on top of the regular Ultra, he used the JG fertilizer with sod - that actually makes the seed germinate faster (without it, it can take three weeks to germinate and take off) and grow in - it's really astonishing.  This seed will have four feet of root system so it shouldn't go dormant in the summer.  Now, I'm happy to say that I have one of the best lawns in the neighborhood.  So if you are having issues with your lawn, maybe consider changing over to Jonathan Green - it might be your silver bullet!

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with their lawn's improvement after switching to Jonathan Green Ultra and JG fertilizer. They mention faster seed germination, better root development, and overall improved grass health, indicating a positive experience.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la satisfacción del usuario con el producto y su impacto positivo en el crecimiento de la hierba. Se menciona una mejora significativa en la salud del césped.

text: Planted 6 weeks ago and have great success with germination. I seeded around Sept 5th and have had plenty of rain and now hope it will survive the maine winter. we had 2 frost mornings and grass growth has slowed to a crawl. I used a garden weasel to loosen dirt in area I was going to lay seed and it works well because it is narrow and gets in between patches of good grass and goes down a inch in the dirt to roughen it up. Also used a thin layer of peat moss in sunny areas as a mulch and had good success with this method just remember to water a little longer to get down to seed because peat absorbs the water first before it gets to the seed. I liked this seed and wish I used it the first time in hot sunny areas near the curb and driveway before I bought blue grass  and layed that down. I think blue grass would do better in half sun locations like north and west protected areas of lawn that get 4 -5 hrs of shade.  The test date of the seed was Jan 2018 and was a little worried of the age of the seed. Had close to 100% germ rate so had no issue with that.  If it survives next spring this is my go to seed for now on .

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with the germination success, mentions good results from using a garden weasel and peat moss as mulch, and indicates that they will be using this seed again next spring. The overall tone is positive, indicating a successful experience.

text:
This is one of those things that's hard to review. As it will be awhile before the true value of it will germinate.

My initial impressions.

Easy to understand instructions about care and maintenance.
A good amount of seed in the bag. I'm not sure exactly how much space it will cover, and fill out, but a 5lb bag has a lot more seed in it than I thought it would.

If you are looking for grass seed make sure to look through the options that are available and pick the one that best suits the area of the country that you live in to provide optimal results.

Since this is something that will take time to fully come into it's own I will periodically update this review with how things are going, and adjust the Star number accordingly if needed.

text: I normally don't write reviews, but the description stated growth a minimum of 12 days. I got my order august 10, spread the seed a day or two later after aerating soil, watered a lot  and on the 18th noticed grass sprouting well. Only on the aerated soil tho. Some soil didn't get done as it was too hot and I was tired. Grass IS coming in there though, but not as quickly as soil that was filled with a spade. I'm so pleased with that that I ordered another bag last night. I have just a few large spots in my yard that I want to fill in, so dispersed the seed by hand. I believe some of the 'blue' was fertilizer, so want to apply more actual seed. Both fertilizer and seed look blue, so apply abundantly which is something I discovered. Will apply more. Good product!!

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with the product's performance, mentioning that grass sprouted well on aerated soil and even in some areas where soil was not filled properly due to hot weather. The reviewer is so pleased with the results that they ordered another bag immediately after seeing positive outcomes.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: El usuario expresa satisfacción con el producto, destacando la rapidez de crecimiento y la eficacia del fertilizante.

phi4-mini.justificacion: La reseña muestra satisfacción general con el producto de jardinería, destacando la buena germinación del semilla y la decisión de comprar otra bolsa.

text:
This is darn good grass seed. They were right. It is easy to use. I put it in a troubled spot of my lawn where it is very dry and nothing wants to grow. Well this seed did the trick. It grew. It took about two weeks for the little grass blades to show and then they took off. It would be great to use to fill any bare spot in your lawn. It has two different kinds each of Fescue, Bluegrass and Ryegrass seed in the mix. I am not a fan of Ryegrass but it will be okay for my one trouble spot I think.
This grass mix is cool because it has a brown dirt like covering already in the mix that lets you know when it needs to be watered by changing from a darker brown to a lighter brown color. You give it a drink and its good for a few days. A plus for us dummies who either forget or do not know when to water. It also has a fungicide in it which helps to grow unhindered. One other good thing about it is the money back guarantee if you are not satisfied with it. Its not something to cover your whole lawn with because you would have to buy too many of them. I recommend it for anyone who just needs to fill a spot in their lawn.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with the grass seed, mentioning that it grew quickly and effectively filled an area previously unsuitable for growth. The product's ability to provide a brown cover indicating watering needs is also highlighted as beneficial. Additionally, the money-back guarantee adds value, making it suitable for small areas or those who might forget to water.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la eficacia del producto para rellenar espacios vacíos en el césped, con una buena mezcla de tipos de hierba y un sistema de riego inteligente. La garantía de devolución también aumenta la confianza.

text:
Imagine sprinkling the fluff that builds up in the dryer vent over your lawn. This is very much what it felt like to put this Repair Mix out --like compressed laundry leftover fluff.

Given this fact I was really curious to see how this product performed. And so far, out of the variety of Scotts and other lawn seeding products it has worked the best.

What I like it that it stayed put and the grass sprouted. Right after I put out my samples the over-night sprinkle that the weatherman had called for, turned into a gully washer. This Repair mix was used in a hilly area and it stayed put.

The patch mix also kept the seed moist.

Sort of pricey for what you get but it works and I'd recommend it if you can find it at a price that suits you.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña indica que el producto se ha mantenido en su lugar después de lluvias intensas y ha proporcionado una buena germinación de semillas, lo cual es un indicador positivo de la eficacia del producto. Además, el usuario recomienda el uso del producto.

text: [[VIDEOID:7acc6cb2d18e24003c2656c67dd7bd0e]] I felt like this was a little finicky for grass seed and the directions were vague. For example, it said how much water to use, but it did not say what amount of seed that was supposed to apply to. People are NOT exaggerating when they say don’t put very much water in this. On my first attempt, the dirt dried out and cracked, and nothing really grew except mold on the surface.  I was much more careful to barely put any water in the second try, and it did a lot better. However, my cat likes to dig in the dirt of house plants, so I purposely planted this pretty thick hoping he wouldn’t be able to get to the dirt as easily. I would say only about half the seeds grew the second time. Some of them worked their way to the surface, so you could see seeds lying on top of the dirt. I didn’t really feel like I could re-cover them, because other grass had already started growing.  My cat loved it, so that’s good, but I will probably try a different brand next time to see if other brands are less temperamental.

text: Providing a review is not my style, but this grass seed is exceptional.  Growing a lawn in the mountains of Western North Carolina has been difficult.  After nine years with three failed lawns of various Fescue and or Bluegrass sod, the lawn was dying again.  This particular seed was added last Fall over the all but dead lawn. text:
I used this in two spots in my yard, both are shady areas. The first was in my front yard to fill in about 4-5 spots that were bare due to lack of light and who knows what (we moved into our house a year ago and this is the first time I've been able to tackle the yard). The second is in the side yard next to a wood fence and concrete patio and under a small tree, very shady and wet area.

I prepped the areas per the instructions on the container and applied the product on a sunny day that was about 65 degrees. I watered immediately after until the water was standing for a few seconds before being soaked up by the product.

After two weeks I'm seeing results. There are thin grass blades coming up all over. They are dispersed, but look really good. The filler/dirt that makes up most of the product is still there, although it doesn't soak up water as much as it did initially.

Overall I'd say this is doing what it's supposed to do. I put up before and after pictures.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer mentions seeing results after two weeks, with thin grass blades coming up that look good despite the filler/dirt still being present but not soaking up water as much. The overall satisfaction is positive.

text:
I put this product down in two different locations. The first is an area where my dogs have killed the grass by urinating on it all the time. The other was a bare spot.
My yard is a more sandy soil, I sprinkled some on the bare spot and covered it with a thin layer of sand. Watered it then let nature take over since we've been getting spring time rains. There is some green sprouting up. Since we have a lawn service (Ga weeds are tough to control!), if there are weeds, they'll be taken care of.
With the dead area, I just sprinkled some around the area, sprinkled some sand on the area, watered once, and again letting nature finish with the watering. Because Bermuda grass is thick like a carpet, I can't really see anything growing yet. But, if the bare spot has new growth, I am assuming that there is new growth in this area also and I just can't see it yet.
My entire yard, which is bermuda grass, is still in the process of turning green from the winter season.
After trying Scotts bermuda grass seed last year, I know that it's a slower growing process with bermuda grass. I trust Scotts products so know that the seed will keep growing and filling in the few areas I have trouble with.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña describe una experiencia generalmente satisfactoria, con algunas áreas donde el producto no ha funcionado como esperado. Sin embargo, la mayoría de las observaciones son positivas y no evidencia insatisfacción ni mala experiencia.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña describe un proceso de siembra y crecimiento, pero no expresa una opinión clara sobre la satisfacción con el producto.

phi4-mini.justificacion: La reseña muestra una mezcla de resultados, con áreas verdes emergiendo y confianza en el producto, pero también reconoce la dificultad del crecimiento lento del pasto bermuda.

text: Purchased in July last summer to quick start a 2" by 25' (300") or a 50 square foot strip of land between an existing lawn & a brick garden wall. I finally had to augment the Hydro Mousse application with some store bought grass seed to fill in the missing grass areas.  Two things are obvious:  1) The product does not work as advertised. 2) The price of $20.00 for a 10' by 10' seeded area is obscene.  For $20/order the coverage should be at least 50' by 50'. text: Great clovers!!! This stuff is a life saver! I had all kinds of dead spots on my grass because my small backyard is north facing and simi blocked from the sun because of the house and trees. I ended up buying four or five of these bags and sprinkled the seeds over the dead spots. Then I covered the top of them with peat moss and watered twice a day during summertime. They grew within a week or so and are still there even into winter! I will buy more in springtime. So awesome! My landlord was very impressed! Buy a good amount because you will probably end up using more than you thought you would.

text: [[VIDEOID:6091658a4639a83ee07b7a6cbcfa7cd4]] I received the seeds in record time for an excellent price! I had to plant the seeds myself and they grew in a couple of days. The seeds didn't come with a label or directions, but the website does list the directions in the product description below. It said to fill a shallow bowl/pot with moist potting soil, sprinkle oats over the soil and lightly press the seeds into the soil. Then just set it on a windowsill to get plenty of light, and keep it evenly moist, but not wet or dry. Germination is in about 5 days. I definitely would recommend this product. I have a video of my cat eating the cat grass. He loves it and can't get enough of it. I'm so happy I bought it!

text: I had previously got and used a pound bag of switch grass seed.  I spread it directly on the prepared ground and got disapointing results with only a few plants growing.  This year I got this smaller amount of seed and planted about 20% in containers and the rest directly in the ground.  The containers had nearly 100% germination and later transplanted well.  The direct seeding had less success, but I have as many plants as I did the previous year with much less effort and expense.  Overall, the seeds germinated well and I am happy.  I will do this again next year.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer mentions that they got nearly 100% germination in containers, which resulted in a higher success rate compared to direct seeding. text: I did not seem to have very good result with this grass. It didn't seem to grow as fast as some of the reviewers claimed it would. In fact, I saw very little new growth at all. I also had a severe outbreak of weeds including many that my grass had never experienced before. I have been pulling these weeds for over a year now. I have almost repaired the damage that these seeds seem to have caused. No, I am not a landscaping professional, but I pride myself on learning the proper techniques for everything i do. I might be willing to try this product again, but in an isolated area to test first, instead of using it to seed the entire lawn in the spring.

text: I've been using these seeds for years. They are reliable and popular with my cats (which is the most important thing, of course).  They sprout relatively quickly.  I usually let them grow to 5-6" before I give them to the cats, but you can make your own decision, of course.  I try to start a new pot right after I give the one that's ready to the felines.  One thing to bear in mind is that if your cats see the plants while they are still short, you may find that they are unwilling to wait and will go after them.  I try to put the new pot in a place where the plants  can get sunlight but aren't too visible to the critters.

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: La reseña indica que los semillas son reliable y populares con las mascotas, lo cual sugiere una satisfacción general. Además, se menciona que la planta crece relativamente rápido y se puede manejar según el usuario deseche. La disposición de la reseña muestra un entusiasmo por la experiencia del producto.

text: I used this off of my back patio where I have not been able to grow anything. I was wanting something there to stop the dirt from getting all over my patio and have been trying for 3 years with no success. I did my homework and read reviews of all available products for a week and a half, so when I finally found this product that said that it does well in any soil I decided to try it and so very glad I did. I live in Murrells Inlet, South Carolina, 2 1/2 miles from the ocean and have very sandy soil. I turned the soil added fertilizer and organic peat after I ordered the seeds, so I was ready to plant them when they arrived (which was 3 days before it was supposed to). When I was ready to put the seeds down I raked the soil and spread the seeds in a small area (because if it didn’t grow I would have sent the rest back) . Within 3 days I had sprouts and it is growing amazingly so I did another area after that and had the same results. Yesterday I did the rest of the area that I was wanting to do. Some other reviews said about mixing the seeds with a filler so you could see where they are, but with this product you don’t need to do that because they are coated with a blue coating that helps retain moisture. Excellent product would highly recommend

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The reviewer expresses satisfaction with the product's performance, mentioning successful growth of seeds in their sandy soil and multiple areas where sprouts appeared within a few days. The blue coating for moisture retention is also highlighted as an advantage.

gemma2:2b.justificacion: La reseña destaca la satisfacción del usuario con el producto, que logró resultados positivos en un suelo difícil de cultivar.

phi4-mini.justificacion: La reseña expresa una excelente experiencia con el producto, destacando la rápida germinación de las semillas y su recomendación para otros.

text: We planted this seed one week ago.  We were primarily filling in very large areas of totally no grass.  We dug up the ground to about 4 inches.  We spread the seed - probably twice as much as it says. We raked the seed into the dirt so most of it was covered. We have been careful to keep the ground moist.  We purposefully planted the seed when it was hot.  I read some on the internet that this seed germinates better in warm soil.  We planted the middle of August.  It's been 7 days and there are already sprouts! I'm so excited.  I can't wait to see how much is germinated in two weeks!! I'll update more later!

text:
This is an effective solution to taking care of fertilizing and overseeding in one step. The blend of fertilizer, grass seed and soil conditioner is easy to spread -- there were zero clumps or issues that interfered with using this with a spreader. For small areas or tight spots it can be hand-spread without problems.

Provided that the ground is prepared before spreading (mowed low and then raked to ensure good ground contact with seed) and then maintained after spreading (regular misting/light watering to keep the seed moist) this works well. I was able to see the results of the fertilizer pretty quickly, and there was some thickening in a few weeks.

The thickening was not as much as I had hoped for since the seed content of the bag is pretty low (about 5% according to the label). There's not much seed to cover the rated 1,200 square feet that this bag is supposed to cover. I needed to do a second over-seeding pass of just seed to get the fill-in that I wanted.

Still, this is certainly a time saver for those who want to fertilize and over seed. It's more expensive than purchasing seed and fertilizer separately, but convenience always adds to the price. My only significant complaint, and the reason for a loss of 1 star, is that the grass seed concentration should be increased to at least double of what it presently is to provide for more in-filling.

text:
If this grass seed isn’t in your cart already, it should be after this review. I don’t have enough good things to say about BBU or Johnathan Green’s products in general. As you can see my backyard was in very bad shape after the summer and years of neglect. Crabgrass, dead grass, broadleaf weeds and bare patches everywhere. I live in southern Connecticut (zone 7a) and we’ve had some record hot days this past summer. This was the year that I decided enough is enough and that it was time to attempt my first seeding project. I started in early August by killing as many weeds as I could with a mixture of tenacity and speed zone (highly recommend) and spot sprayed the mature crabgrass with glyphosate.

By the end of August the lawn looked like a battlefield, probably the worst I’ve ever seen it. The weekend of September 4th I aerated, de-thatched and seeded with BBU, Mag-I-cal plus by Johnathan Green, and Lesco’s 18-24-12 starter fert. Four days later I saw the very first seedling and around day 5 or 6 baby grass was popping up everywhere. Somewhere between weeks 2 and 3 a huge tree branch actually fell on my yard from the neighbors tree (they tried to sabotage me!). I thought for sure the lawn would be damaged from the branch itself, the shade from the leaves, and the guys who had to walk on the lawn to remove it. But this grass is tough, IT SURVIVED. By the 3 week mark the grass was already approaching 6 inches in some areas so I had to mow it.

A month after seeding the lawn is greener and thicker than its ever been! Everyone in the house wants to walk [...]

qwen2.5:1.5b.justificacion: The review indicates a significant improvement in the homeowner's yard, with grass growing despite challenging conditions such as crabgrass, dead grass, and bare patches. The reviewer mentions that they started their project after years of neglect and had to deal with various issues like weed control and tree branches falling on the lawn. Despite these challenges, the grass survived and thrived within a few weeks. The homeowner's satisfaction is evident in the positive tone and the statement that everyone in the house wants to walk through the yard.

text: I planted a 15' x 15' area of my lawn with Pearl's Premium.  I used a roto-tiller to remove the weeds and dead grass before planting.  I planted in Mid-October (2012) which is acceptable for my zone 7.  By November, there was great coverage, and that section of my lawn really established itself the following year.  In Spring of 2014, I planted another 5x2' section from this same batch of seed, and that is absolutely perfect to this day.  Since then, I've seeded other areas of my lawn, with other brands and they simply did not perform as well as Pearl's premium.  In the areas where Pearl's Premium is used, there are far less weeds, and simply put they are the showcase of my yard.  The company states that the seed is good for 18 months (I emailed them), and I tried to plant my remaining seed after 36 months and unfortunately the company is right, and this 3rd batch did not germinate.  So here I am, back to buy more, and I'm glad to see that Amazon carries this product now

text: First of all, the sellers do not include an instruction manual with the product so you have to go on line to find out what to do with seeds and growing material ..... since when is that a good business decision? Others have reported their frustration with the green screen through which the grass is supposed to grow through in that it pops up because the grass pushes it.  Here's another problem with the design:  My kitty starting pulling the sprouting seeds out so I put the screen on and she figured out how to pull it up so she could get to the seeds.  Why didn't the designers think about this and put a lip on the screen so that it could be locked in place? I really thought Catit was better than this product.

text: Turf type tall fescue (TTTF)is highly recommended by most state AG extension depts for lawns in the so-called transition zone, due to its drought tolerance and quick establishment. However, be aware that it does have significant limitations. The drought tolerance, which comes from its ability to root deeply, can be poor in clay soil, which is very common all over the country, but particularly in the Mid-Atlantic region, much of which is in the transition zone. The color is a dark green, but the blades are not as fine as Kentucky bluegrass, and they tend to get even wider as the grass ages, and if the turf starts to thin out, they begin to look like K-31. You also need to overseed bare spots constantly, because it is a bunch type grass that does not spread by rhizomes the way KBG does. It is also a significantly more prone to fungus than KBG, a mjor downside in the humid parts of the country. Finally, it does not really go dormant in high heat/drought the way KBG does -- it survives because it finds water in the deep roots. If the roots are not deep, like in clay soil, it will be toast above about 90F. So I would say this -- if you do not have clay soil, this is going to be a great choice. But if your soil usually feels brick hard in summer, meaning it has a lot of clay, I'd try either KBG or perennial ryegrass, which do much better in compacted soils.
